reviews.review_description:
1 star because we're all conned into buying half a game it say part one but the studio has shut down, so there isn't a part 2 every single vr player that bought this game and the addon should be compensated.


as of early May 2026, Survios—the veteran VR studio behind Raw Data, Creed: Rise to Glory, and Alien: Rogue Incursion—has reportedly shut down following massive layoffs

Alright its been a bit over a year since its release, i bought this game on sale, and my first play through was roughly 8 hours. As an update if you aren't big on the game dev cycle and world, Survios the studio in charge has let go most of their dev team. If you heard any of the release drama, the game was broken into 2 parts meaning that we may not be getting a part 2, or at least from the same studio. With that all as a precursor I still recommend this game but hopefully you get it on sale or you might feel like you've been stiffed. Dont let the price affect how you manage your expectations.

This game was a blast on stand alone. People complain about the graphics and its true, they aren't the best compared to behemoth, metro, or batman. But you dont play this game because of the graphics, you play this game to feel like you are in ALIENS! NOT ALIEN by Riddley Scott but the sequel by James Cameron. Yes i have stutters playing on a base quest 3, the headset gets hot, and the battery does drain fast. With all of those warts I still have not encountered anything game breaking. 

For the Alien nerds out there, the story is great. Doesn't break Canon or continuity. Lore text logs and audio are well acted. There are no skippable cutscenes and what characters and documents say are actually intertwined with objectives so make sure to pay attention or you might get confused or lost on where to go. I'd say more but no, play the game! If not WolfinVR does have a full playthrough for you. 

Mechanically this game is NOT like Alien Isolation so anyone looking for a slow, methodical, & tense game, go play Alien isolation. Technically is can be slow and methodical if you're a scardy cat like me who is role-playing as a dumb marine and not Zula but those moments, when you truly immerse yourself and use the motion tracker slow with a hand Canon, are what make the game memorable. The game also throws a lot of aliens at you pretty off the bat so it is very much ACTION and not horror focused. The game has a RE typewriter like save system which can be a blessing and a curse. The map is surprisingly big with thoughtful back tracking. Mini games with the clamp and the torch are actually fun and mostly optional. There isnt a huge array of weapons but its got the classic rifle, shotgun, pistol so you dont really need more. No enemy variety but thats the baggage of the IP. 

This game often feels like im playing a flat game in VR. Little things like aiming, the full body rig, the camera sway. Its the most I've ever felt like i was in a FPS while I was in VR rather than playing a VR shooter, if that makes sense to anyone.

Even at full price I think this game is worth it. At 40 its less than 10$ an hour if you dont do replays or speedeuns. Wait for a sale as im sure they will come and go to get an even better return. I hope Part 2 gets made but without the team that made part 1 its looking highly unlikely.

reviews.review_title: Aliens in vr is what it is...
reviews.review_description:
The game is everything you'd expect in a vr game based on the aliens franchise. I expected the graphics to be not pc level on quest but I thought they'd be better. Or at least Arkham shadows level. At times the xenos seem pixilated when they move (bad view distance). Other than that the game runs smooth and and the gameplay is satisfying. I turned off the snap to eye aiming as I like immersion in vr but the collision with self seems off a bit. Got to the part where you got to grab davids head but disappointed you can't stow him somewhere and always have to drop and find him when fighting. Mostly it's bug killing fun and suspenseful aliens feel so it is what you expect. If anything this needs a bit off graphics polish to smooth things over and more darkness shading if possible for atmosphere.

Tempted to remove a star for it being shorter then expected. I know they said it would be part 1 but still a full sized game. It is not. Typical for quest size maybe but it is cutoff halfway through with no eta on part 2. It's propagation: paradise hotel all over again.
 Removed that star cuz I don't think part 2 is coming. If it's not secured dont end on a teaser.

The notion that you can't have teleport movement or something similar because the gameplay has to be 'hardcore righteous, dude' is absolute balls. 

30-40 percent of the VR community suffer from motion sickness, but you won't find their comments here cos you've got to buy the sodding game to comment!

So, I'll take one for the queasy team.

Vignetting DOESNT WORK! It's never worked. The only thing that works, is teleport motion, which has been well implemented in Res Evil and Assassin's Creed, and if those games can do it, this simple, simple Doom like corridor hike could have managed as well, but the devs got lazy.

So, 40% of the Quest family can't play this game. 

Enjoy those low scores and sales, fellas. VR sales are down, you say? Gee, if only you could boost them 30 or 40 percent. Duuurrr. 

No one is saying, take away full motion. We don't want to remove anything, we just want to be included. The nauseous 40% have money as well, you know. 

Anyway, I can't play, just bought it to comment and give a nice low, excluded from playing, one star!

reviews.review_title: Game had potential but missed it
reviews.review_description:
So first of all the game will never be finished or have part 2 because of the layoffs so..just FYI before buying the game but I will rate it for what it is right now 
 I played it for a while and now my view kind of changed still not great though 

So about the visuals I say they're okay like for the quest they are okay the lighting is bad I must add especially on the characters but other than that there is a little bit of stutter especially in a few places the fire graphics are laughable but it's possible so overall I would say it's fine 

The combat the combat is okay too don't expect alien isolation style the AI are okay not groundbreaking or something like that the the guns are fine the shotgun though it's very nice but overall I would say it's passable 

The voice acting at first I hate it the snarky character marks in serious situation but then it kind of grew on me but I love you what you expect the story without spoilers I would say generic possible 

The map is quite good I must say I like the design of the base and surroundings in the jumpscares are not too much

Basically overall I would say it's a possible game so if you get it for very cheap it's quite good but full price no but it's not making me super mad and i quite enjoy it

reviews.review_title: Alright, sweethearts! You know the drill
reviews.review_description:
I have to say, I'm new to VR, but I am definitely not new to gaming. I recently got a Quest 3S and this was one of the first games I got. I just completed it and I have to say it is one of my favourite games on VR so far (in the time of getting this i have sampled quite a lot of games through Horizon+)! 
Now there is some lacking in the graphics, but what do you expect for putting such an ambitious game on pretty much a phone in your face?! I see people complaining on the graphics, but despite some rendering it is still decent and with a lot of detail! It's not a massive playspace but they use and reuse it all really well!

There is a real atmosphere and the sounds are impressive! The action is exciting and once you get the hang of it you can dance around well. I don't really get any motion sickness from this game.

The mechanics are mostly clean and substantial when you use them. All the equipment on your body is easy to access and the movements quite intuitive. The only struggling is grabbing things from boxes, where your arm can get awkwardly stuck and cause a bit of a wild flapping situation. Or if you try to duck and pick something up and comedically accidentally kick it away from yourself. But they aren't major issues. I like the idea of items being in boxes and such!

The xenos are quite relentless. "Always 2 there are"...mostly...at a time. They can surprise you, circumvent around you and surprise you, even though there is only one main type on top of facehuggers. Now they do appear a bit too regularly which can make it difficult when you're trying to read messages or do puzzles. But at the same time, appearing regularly makes sure that you never let your guard down and it never gets dull. Attacking so often gives a sense of urgency to tasks and unpredictability. They do actually find creative ways to attack and approach you, which keeps you on your toes! The weapons have satisfying kickback and power to them, and it does depend where you hit the enemies.
You have a companion helping you at one point which is really cool, and I must say that he did the main grunt work.
You have to carry something at one point. This can seem annoying but also it means that you have to take a bit more care, and it isn't forever that you have to carry it either.

The climbing and interactions work really well! And even the wiring puzzles are interesting when you get the hang of them! Breaking the welds on doors is satisfying, as well as picking up items and lore pieces that keep the story engaging. Even applying meds. Now meds and ammo don't respawn but there is plenty around if you look for it!
The voice acting is great! Don't listen to people that hate on it! It is really good, especially between the main two characters and even all the voice notes left around!

It's a longer campaign than I expected! Just as I thought it was gonna end it opened up more! Now it's not significant like a flat screen length game, but it is still beefy for a VR game! Especially if you space out your playtime. I did about half an hour session, did a section then saved and came back the next day or so. And it lasts. Also saving is nicely old skool which adds to the fear element. Not many auto saves and have to go to certain rooms to manually save. I will suggest using these whenever you can and especially after using an elevator (because those sections have significant load times)!

All in all this is genuinely a fantastic game. People need to stop hating on it for not being perfect and for expecting insane levels of graphics on a platform like the Quest. It is great that we have it and it is still incredible! I'm glad the devs made this and we need more efforts like this! Aliens is one of my favourite films, and I've played most of the games. This is definitely one of the best, and I'm not just saying that! Support this game! Just don't have impossible expectations for it. Devs, please we need that part two!!

reviews.review_title: Poorly optimized, needed more dev time
reviews.review_description:
Edit: after refunding the game, I bought it again on sale for $10, I can recommend the game at this price. The game actually runs a bit better, will further update my review as I play more. 


I've been playing quest games since the two came out, and I have to say that this is the most poorly optimized quest game I have ever played. I read the reviews comparing the Quest 3 versions to the others, so I knew there would be some compromises, but this is ridiculous. My game crashed 3 times before even getting through the tutorial. I am not faulting the development team since they were probably forced to push the game out before it was ready, even though the quest version was delayed. I have never requested a refund for a quest game, but I think I will be doing so for this one. I know the devs said they would be putting out a patch next week, but it sounds like it's too little too late; a game that was delayed shouldn't launch with this level of problems. I was excited about this game, but my first impression was horrible. The game is also slow to start, and the graphics are muddy.

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR games I played
reviews.review_description: I LOVE this game, it's so much fun! Even though I don't particularly like the story, I find it incredible whether you're a fan of the franchise or not. I also feel like the ending was a bit forced, but aside from that, I think it's one of the best VR games ever made, and I don't understand why people complain about the graphics; I think they're great.

reviews.review_title: Feels like the movie, but big flaw.
reviews.review_description: It's a good game, enjoyable. The only thing that is frustrating is that there are not enough save points throughout the game. I spent 20 minutes getting through an area, plenty of computer terminals in the section but not one save point. Had to stop playing to do something and it went right back to the first save point. I cannot understand why there isn't one at the end of each major stage. I will keep the game, but this is a big negative.

The game could be great if there were no bugs or glitches. The graphics are not that bad, the game has a story, it keeps you constantly tense. However, it happens during the game that the game simply freezes and you lose all the time after saving, so save constantly, do part of the mission, go back to the panic room and save because otherwise you will lose your temper. Choose a normal or easy challenge, otherwise you will lack ammo at every step. After half the game, the map will not give you the exact locations, which can cause you problems. I decided on 4/5, although I am disappointed because the sequel was never released even though the developers promised it. The game is worth your time, save often and you will make many mistakes in the game easier.

This was so much fun very few VR games like Batman Arkham Shadows and this one sure there were a few times I try to equip my plus rifle or try to fine were to go overall it is one of the best dark atmosphere looking up at ceilings behide corners going to vents I like the combat and story I give it 5 stars can’t wait for part ll when ever it’s coming when it’s on sale aliens were creepy on the walls and all if anyone wants a game in there library it’s this game right here.

This game should not have been ported to the Quest 3. Barely PS2 level graphics, so dark you can’t see and the audio has no depth. The treble will pierce your eardrums. The gameplay is buggy, the xenos just show up randomly with no context. The actor that voices the protagonist can’t act. Just a terrible game all around.

this is a great story like no other game i've ever played in my life i've had the best time in this game i don't think my friends have ever played this game and if they did i bet you they would also rate this game of five star the only downside about the game is the safe system i like how it is manual save but if there was some auto save that would be great i played through the whole campaign it was a great game i highly recommend anyone who wants a survival horror game i get this because the xenomorphs in this game are giant i was hoping for a game like this for years and we finally got one

reviews.review_title: 5 Star Game on PSVR2, bad Quest Port
reviews.review_description:
Bought this for my Quest 3 and graphics and frame rate drops were so bad that I couldn’t bring myself to play past the first 15 minutes.  Think PS2 graphics, frame rates and lighting.  Tried with and without QGO on various settings - all bad.

I’m such an avid Alien fan that I ended up purchasing this game again on PSVR2 and it’s absolutely amazing on that platform.  Lighting and Atmosphere are top notch.  Graphics and Performance are flawless.  Ties into the Alien universe nicely and references Alien Isolation several times.  Some players have complained about lack of save points, and that’s by design to add another layer of stress to the survival aspect of this game and made sense to me.  Get good noobs.

I gave this game 5 stars on PSVR2, and 3 stars on Quest.  It probably should not have been ported to Quest at all frankly.
Excellent voice acting.  it is a nice game but gets repetitive. Thanks for the story mode.  Maps are not easy to understand.  Too many of the same electric box.  Ends on a PART 2 is coming !
Wow such a great gaming experience it challenged my brain multiple times, i cant wait for part 2 i will definitely buy it!

The weapon handling is terrible. The map system is terrible. You have to constantly save the game, and only in certain locations—there’s no autosave. The Alien itself might be well designed, but its attack behavior is absurd. The game doesn’t feel like a horror game at all.
It's not the worst but I wish we got different variants of xenos then just the regular ones, they get boring after awhile
This game at launch was not great but with the hotfixs it’s better than ever. Please make a sequel to this game!
Best Aliens game ever!!!

The game is so good and so cool I love that we get to see the face huggers and xenomorph and you literally don’t know if their behind you or not and I like that when you kill the face huggers and your free of that vein chair and your in that cave thing! Definitely a 5 star highly recommend!

The game is all right, I guess, but it didn't amaze me. I expected to feel uneasy at least and never did. I chased down xenomorphs in order to see one after it was dead because they quickly despawn after being killed, and they take forever to path to you anyway. In the end, I just wanted to see the end and be done. The story and pacing bored me.

We've already seen from alien colonial marines that this shooting gallery formula for an alien game doesn't work we should have had an alien isolation vr. It seems like they wanted to do that because this game has a saving system just like isolatio. Not only is this game disappointing but it also just doesn't run well on the quest i'll finish this game then never play it again Sorry.

You should be able to test the games before you buy. I didn’t like how the mechanics worked. Also the game is very heavy and frame rate drops. 
The aliens are also not really good enemies, they should have a better mix of human and robot enemies and fewer aliens.

I want my money back!

reviews.review_title: Bug at the start of the game...
reviews.review_description:
... so I am not interessted in continue with all the bugs reported I read afterwards.

Thing is at the very beginning, I have to pick up a suringe. The pick up works and  ejecting the needle works but I can't inject it. The tutorial still asks to eject the needle even so the needle is already ejected. Nothing goes afterwards. I was three minutes in game and this bug already broke it for me. Can not imagine what else will broke the game if this already happens at start.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, 

Apologies for this issue you experienced with the game.  Whenever you have a chance, could you please contact our support team at support@survios.com so we can troubleshoot this issue with you?

Thank you again for your patience!

reviews.review_description:
I have tried this and abandoned this game so many times by now. 
It has so much potential. It looks pretty good, it sounds really good, has a good story, but it runs like crap!  Frame rate is terrible and it gets really blurry when moving fast. Definitely not for weak stomachs. 
And the click to run mechanic? I hate it in any game, but especially in this. I have probably lost 2 hours to this not always engaging when you need it. 
I will eventually complete it, but I can't say I have totally enjoyed it. 
Try it, if you are a fan of the Alien franchise, but forget it if you are not.

So I like the game and for me there were no graphics drops and lag periods. But can you add more types of aliens. Like make a spitter or a runner it would just spice up game. Otherwise good job. I CANT WAIT UNTIL PART 2!

Game has good athmospehere. Nice weapons. I know there are many minor bugs but i dont care. The game is good for me. The save points made the game difficult. No need every corner a checkpoint. I am looking forward the chapter 2.
Watched the last 10 min of game in Walkthrough as too much repetitive scenes. Overall a decent game but happy completed. Thanks YouTube. 😃

You suck, what's the point of paying all this flipping money for something? I'm only allowed me 3 saves 3 saves. That's it seriously. What's the point of buying the game paying all the flipping money? And you can't use it as you please. They need to put that in the theme before you buy it, not in the eaty, fine print or not. At all, plain straight up forward. So basically you make all this money off people. And they can't play the game, they have to sit there and literally keep replaying replaying.They only allow three saves.Let's say three saves!

I actually think the game has good atmosphere and would be so much fun if not for the horrible save points.  I don't want to have to stop the game because of time constraints or battery issues only to have to start way back when I come back. Because of this I stopped playing early on.

Firstly I have to say well done for making a more diverse game than most using a black female protagonist.

This brings a whole new prospective of people to gaming and thats great by the developers.

On playing the game I found this game smooth and solid.
Some frame rate skips but so what is not a pvp game and actually adds to the emersion!
(Just imagine blinking)

The one annoying thing I found was the mist at the bottom of the screen when leaving the play area, not the guardian but play area. Peaking round corners is made hard with this but with a little tweaking this its not a problem.

Aiming is just as good down the sight as most top FPS games in VR, this is a great try at bringing the old Sega title into VR.

I played every single alien game that existed, and this is the best one for ME, let me explain.

VR, the game is insanely good in virtual reality, they put a lot of interation with the world and objects, you are not just walking around, you literaly unlock rooms, bypast security protocol, you really feel like you are there.

The mechanics, when you crouch, run ou shoot, the weight of the gun is super realistic, the character dosent feal like he is independant from the physic and the world like other games

THE SOUND ! every step can alert nearby alien, when you talk in dialogue you can hear the echo in the room, something i have not seen yet in other games, the sound of aliens, the detectors, every sound you have to listen and its perfect, give chills many times

The openworld environement, you can explore, come back and unlock rooms you couldn't erlier in the game, for a stanalone game the map is huge, and its not a walk in the park, if you don't check your step you end up in a corner with 4 of them that followed you before planning am attack

In all, the game is perfect, i didnt read the comments before buying, came back after 2 hours and after and i regret nothing, yes the save system is something because you have to think about it, its some safe room where you can save, if you dont go amd die you loose progress, but alion isolation used that system and ppl loved it, so charge your quest before playing, and enjoy the thrill ride
the game was fun to play when i first started it. but now that i've gone back to it to try and replay it it won't launch just crashes. i don't understand why because i have a meta quest 3

developer_response.body:
Hi there, Apologies for this black screen issue you're experiencing with the game. Whenever you have a chance, could you please contact our support team at support@survios.com so we can troubleshoot this issue with you?

Thank you for your patience!

reviews.review_description: Thoroughly enjoyed . I just loved the experience of alien in vr being a fan of the movies and was very pleased with the experience . Only some bugs but overall great. Cant wait for part 2 ! 5 stars !!!

I have both the pcvr and this stand alone and to run native on the Quest is pretty awesome.
Can't compare the visuals to the pc version but its definatly good for running natively.
If they would polish it more up it would have been nice but I guess they are done after the big update to fix the terrible release version.
I only got this after the update so was on the fence for long u till they updated it i took the chance in a sale and wasn't disappointed.
I did tweak it a bit via game Optimiser and it runs pretty good.

Is it scary? Eh, more like unsettling, like you don't know when 5 aliens might pop around a corner. At the same time, you're given several weapons to absolutely decimate these things, like more aliens will die in this game then in any of the movies. Decent story, decent gunplay, decent graphics, and a lot of details that don't get put into most vr games. This game really feels like it's trying to be a well put together vr experience, besides the jank of course. I think it does most things pretty well, and is worth a try.

Rogue Incursion returns to the style of the old AVP titles, in which we encounter a station completely infested with xenomorphs.
We slip into the shoes of Zula Hendricks, a former member of the Colonial Marines. Together with our synthetic companion “Davis 01,” we travel to the planet LV-354 “Purdan” to infiltrate a GES research facility. The game world has been designed to be very atmospheric. At the beginning, many of the station's doors and storage cabinets are locked and can only be opened with specific key cards or tools. In many areas, the power has also failed and must be reactivated. With new key cards and tools, we can gradually advance further into the station. To save our game progress, we have to find so-called “panic rooms.” Similar to “Isolation,” we have to dock our PDA to special terminals. If an alien is nearby, the terminal displays an error message and we cannot save until the danger has been eliminated. Graphically, the game only looks OK on the Quest. The music is, of course, based on the films and has been authentically composed. And the most important thing in an Alien game, the frightening soundscape, has been perfectly implemented. The iconic sounds of the doors, the alarms, the steam noises, and above all the hissing of the aliens have been brilliantly implemented. The VR mechanics all work flawlessly and feel extremely good. Climbing, pulling switches, turning wheels, inserting ID cards... all these things have great feedback and feel really satisfying. Computers are controlled with a joystick, which we have to grab. We have to rewire fuse boxes with a kind of electric pliers, and welded doors are opened with a mandatory welding torch. Here, we have to drive over a thick weld seam to burn it away. Over time, this creates more and more shortcuts to return to old locations.
Conclusion: It took me a full 9 hours to complete the game. The story is exciting and has a few twists and turns. It all culminates in an epic boss.

reviews.review_title: Nice but gotta keep running back to save
reviews.review_description:
The atmosphere and sound are good. As long as you know you're signing up more for an action game than a sneaking one there's a lot to like about this. I had several frustrating times needing to relisten to a long winded dialog or redo a monotonous task because too many enemies spawned before I could get back to a save room, or once the game randomly crashed which left me thereafter extra frequently saving*. But ultimately I stuck through it until the end and am overall glad I did. Looking forward to part 2.

*After a data transfer and long story sequence I tried sticking a keycard into a nearby yellow door to see if that affected it somehow, instantly crashed the game completely.
I've only been playing for like 15 minutes and I already love it. The only problem is walking speed
just finished the game it was way too good i need a part 2 NOW!
We need a part 2 sooooooo bad
So good try the game
A very good game with good graphics good story plenty of fighting and lots of puzzles to do

UPDATE Sept 2025:

I decided to give the game another go, and couple tell that the devs have been working on it. 

I just completed the game, and have to say, it was awesome! The graphics are still fine, they get the job done The performance is a lot better than when I first reviewed, but still has a little frame rate drop every now and then…but the gameplay design and sound design is what makes this game great. Like another reviewer mentioned, you never feel safe, and it’s a pretty terrifying experience.

If I could compare the feeling I had playing this, I’ve got to go waaaaay back to Resident Evil 1 on the PS1. 

Anyway…thanks Survios for the fixes, and I look forward to Part 2 👍🏻


I’m trying really hard to enjoy this game. I waited to purchase until the new patch was released. Graphics are fine. Not great, and not bad. The problem I have is the performance of the game. The resolution is terrible, and the frame rate just sucks. The physics of the arms and objects are weird. Just not enjoyable. 

As other reviewers have mentioned, and the devs have not responded to…how is this game not optimized. When we have the likes of Batman, Metro, and Behemoth all released within the last 6 months, and older games like AW2, Red Matter 2, Hubris, RE4 to name a few, there really should be no excuse for what Survios released. I’m keeping the game, because I want to support developers, and am hoping the first patch is the first of many.

WILL MAKE YOU WANT TO COMITE SUICIDE IN THE GAME FROM HOW SCARY IT IS.  😵💥🔫

I was playing thinking it was going to be chill at the start but the second I saw one xenomorph I folded my arms tensed up and I did not want to play more but I did but the second I was in the dark with a flashlight and my guns and waves came I wasted all of my ammo and when I couldn’t reload the revolver I died because the ai is actually smart

10/10 horror action game very good
Would die again

(Also the arm hit box the finger physics and the procedural grabbing is good but bugs ruin it a little) 

vr jump scares are more horrifying than any horror movie or flat screen game you will ever play/watch.

-edit. Some people say the save system in this game ruins it but it’s really just a skill issue although it’s not for new people to vr its really annoying loosing a lot of progress but that doesn’t matter if you are good at the game.￼

reviews.review_title: Much better than I was expecting
reviews.review_description: From the reviews, I was expecting the game to be pretty bad, but I actually ended up loving the game. First the bad: there is not much enemy variety, there are some bugs (like sometimes grabbing objects does't work), and it would have been cool to have some bosses mid-game. However, if you like this type of game (suspense shooters in dark corridors), then chances are you will love this game. Graphics are not amazing (don't expect something like Batman) but they are actually decent for the Quest, although there are some jagged edges on things that are not close to you (QGO helps). Performance is also not bad (from the reviews I was expecting to be pretty laggy, but I only encountered laggy situations twice during my entire playthrough and it only lasted for a couple of seconds), so maybe this was improved in the latest patches. The suspense of the game is great, I like how instead of a linear path you have this metroid-vania map style (similar to games like Genotype), the weapons have weight and the gunplay is good (although the rifle could have a bit better ADS), but the best are the interactions that add to the immersion: opening or closing a door/hatch to avoid the aliens is done by pulling a lever, interacting with terminals is really interesting (you move the cursor with a virtual joystick), accessing the map and objectives is done via a touch screen unfoldable gadget that you hold, the puzzles are done using a tool that allows you to connect/disconnect live wires, using the retro-style motion tracker is super cool, and weapon reloading is also well done which adds to tense situations where you are being attacked but need to reload your weapon fast. It's a shame that this game had a rough launch as I think that reflected in the poor score that the game received.
I don't agree with criticism, this is great game, waiting for Pt.2!

reviews.review_title: Is part 2 to be a separate game
reviews.review_description: I’m on my second play through of the game and I’m wondering if part 2 will be sold as a separate game or will it be added to the first game. And also this game is really good at bringing in a terrifying atmosphere and the tension in the air whilst being hunted by the Xeno’s.
It's a good game but I'm getting tired of waiting for part two to come out to finish it. Will be five stars whenever they get that out

When this game released, it was rough. But the Developers  have since updated it. Graphics look great for the meta quest 3. Gameplay is fun and terrifying. The pulse rifle is effective with good aiming and short controlled bursts. Shotgun packs a punch but the zenomorphs are tough. The game never lets you feel safe. Be prepared to use plenty of stims. Difficulty is challenging. I died quite a bit. If you are an alien fan, this vr game is a must. For the folks behind this game, Thank you! Looking forward to part 2.

reviews.review_title: FOR THE LOVE OF GOD ADD AUTOSAVE
reviews.review_description: The amount of times I've had to redo an annoyingly long stretch of gameplay over and over again because there's no save. I don't wanna have to redo 15 minutes of gameplay 5 times just because I keep getting swarmed, especially when i only have so much battery life to play it. i love the game otherwise, but WOW does it need autosave

All the negative reviews had me scared to grab this  game but I'm sure glad I did. The atmosphere and sound effects keep you on the edge of your seat. You are IN the world of Aliens. To me, it's almost perfect. The lighting, the vents, sparks, the darkness, the Aliens, the scare factor have all been well worth the price so far. Because of the negative reviews, I was expecting some clunky, flat surfaced Nintendo 64 type nonsense but this game is fantastic to me. Like I said earlier, you are IN the world of Aliens. The graphics look good to me on the Quest 3. Is it a high end gaming PC look????No, but it IS good. 
My ONLY complaint if it's even really a complaint is the gun mechanics and accuracy take a little getting used to.
 Hold on to the front of it with your off hand and hang on.  Don't try to aim. Just shoot. The Aliens look just like the movie and move accordingly. They come from vents, the ceiling, hidding in the dark....everywhere. 
If you're a fan of the Alien franchise, shooters and creepy survival horror games then  this is a must for your VR library. 👍👍👍👍👍

So I like the game...people complain about the save points but you just have to go to them more often and make sure to save if you progress. The issue is I got back to the ship and the capacitors needed one of which was where the map marked the other is on the map but missing. So now the entire game is ruined. Id have to stsrt again hoping it didnt happen again? What a joke. How can they not fix this for 54 bucks come on. I was so close and not om totally stuck. Anyone have any thoughts on this from the makers please advise before I delete.

developer_response.body:
Hi there,

Apologies for this issue you're experiencing with the game.

Whenever you have a chance, could you please contact our support team at support@survios.com so we can troubleshoot this issue and gather more information from you?

Thank you for your patience!

reviews.review_title: Overall, really good. A couple of things
reviews.review_description:
The gameplay is great although picking things up can time be a little bit difficult… The graphics are very good and the atmosphere and sense of tension is through the roof. 

The storyline is good and ignore the people saying it is just a little bit ’ woke’. ￼ clearly they are the Twitter generation who are triggered by real storylines and real peoples stories. I appreciate the lack of auto save is difficult for some people but it actually enhance the game for me having to get from ATB a few times without dying.


A thing that would definitely be improved is the ability to change the difficulty level during the game, like many other games nowadays… I am about to a quarter of the way through and I’ve had to restart to lower the difficulty level as I can progress after a few days and it gets quite repetitive doing the same section and fear that I would come across this again possibly later on if by some chance I managed to get in the lift (no spoilers)

Fix the difficulty level and the ability to change it mid game and make it easier to pick up things especially in a hurry was being chased by a few aliens… And this would be five stars ￼

Despite some interaction bugs and visual degradation when more than one xenomorph is in view, it's a pretty solid game. The story is engaging, voice acting is solid and they capture the atmosphere of the movies really well. Just hope they figure out how to squeeze a little more juice out of the quest3 to allow for better frame rates and less blur. Also, gun play could be better. Idk if they have autoaim enabled, but either way, it doesn't feel as natural as other games. Outside of that, I really like it and recommend it to anyone who's a fan of the movies.

reviews.review_title: Imagine if ALYX was ported to Quest 3
reviews.review_description: Listen, everybody has opinions. And everybody's entitled to them. Everything is arguable. Everything is subjective. Nobody could ever have the DEFINITIVE opinion on something. SO I'm here just to give you MY thoughts on this absolutely NERVE THRASHING, REALISTIC LIVING NIGHTMARE experience that is this game. Is the resolution of the rendering the same as the PCVR version? Of course not. Shame on you if you thought it would be. Remember the hardware. BUT.. is the ATMOSPHERE, the MUSIC, the clever feeling of WEIGHT of everything you hold, the interaction with almost everything..... THE FEAR.... is THAT ALL THE SAME? Absolutely. I've never played a game thus far that within ONE HOUR my nerves are saying "Ok, that's enough, we can't stay ELEVATED that long..". It's that good. For those of you who say the Aliens attack the same way each time... I'm not entirely sure what game you were playing... each time I played, they would strategize differently depending on YOUR location. My normal strategy of once I know where they're coming from putting as much space between me and them so I can get clear shots... NOPE.. they would LUNGE in one swoop and get me. They crawl out of the vents.. out of the ceiling panels.. out of open windows, doors... you're NEVER relaxed... there's always SOMETHING LURKING and you can HEAR it. If you ever wanted to know what it's like to really LIVE in the ALIEN UNIVERSE... then this experience is all you need. And to explain my review title, I really believe that if someone PORTED HALF LIFE-ALYX (my all time favorite VR game) to the Quest 3 it would NOT be superior to this experience. So if you're looking for maximum resolution through and through then get the PCVR version.. but if you also appreciate NOT NEEDING TO HAVE YOUR PC nearby or even ON and the convenience and speed with with you can begin your adventure then the Quest 3 version holds its own confidently and truly rocks. It's officially my FAVORITE native Quest 3 title ever.
I’ve been looking for a vr experience similar to alyx and returned a few , this one is a keeper!!!!! Very happy works beautifully and is entertaining.

This should have been called Alien Lesbian Incursion. Ridiculous gender politics pushing throughout the game. Give it a rest already. It completely depletes the quality of the writing. The actress who does the voiceover for the lead protagonist is not good and becomes grating early on in the game. The game play involves a lot of backtracking and the same constant mini puzzle to open doors. Not much creativity there.

yeah this game is pretty awesome if you're an alien fan but only if you feel like winning easily.... or not at all. 
these game devs don't know how to make an in the middle.
they are probably one of the groups that was fired by Master Zuckerberg so it doesn't matter what I say they're not going to fix it or care or even read this. 

I loved this game until I played it on hard because normal was way way too easy. 

But hard is impossible! they even have another level after that lol these people just don't know how to make games like I don't know if they don't spend time on it or what but I wouldn't hire them or buy another game from them if my life depended on it.
Loved every min of this game when's next part coming!

Gameplay/story/sound excellent. Feel/look of movies represented well. Decent length approx 8hrs. Standout moments, drool dripping down in front of me made me look up to see an alien on ceiling about to lunge, fending off face huggers with limited ammo, panic inducing boss fight at end. Not sure I understand comments around issues with save points and needing auto save. I have limited time to play (young family) and found the panic rooms (safe zones to save) regularly placed so that I could save every 20 mins if needed. Star knocked off for graphics as while far from game breaking sometimes jagged edges can be a bit distracting, but we are playing on a standalone headset. Looking forward to part 2.

I nearly returned this game after the first time playing, but im so glad I didn’t. Is it frustrating￼ At first tying to pick things up ? YES . Is having to reload while running from the aliens a nightmare  ? YES!
I find it hard reading the map too , but that might just be me . 
Panic sets in hard with this game but I keep coming back for more . Great game

Great story great graphics decent gun physics could be better maybe have a sight upgrade in part 2 hopefully but all around i was not expecting such an open map or how well the game looked truly a must buy

reviews.review_title: THIS IS WHAT VR WAS MADE FOR 🤘👊
reviews.review_description:
This is hands down one of the best VR Games/Experiences !!! 
All those bad reviews are from people who are obviously not that good at games to be leaving reviews in the first place 😪

Don’t let these bad player reviews ruin something amazing! You guys did a fantastic job with this game from top to bottom. 
I love action/Horror games and This is literally the ultimate VR experience in my opinion ! I 

I Iove how players are so bad at a game that they think its the devs fault 😂 like Jeez if you suck at the game just say that🙄


Just remember to manually save your progress whenever you come across new objectives 👍

reviews.review_title: Aliens VR game I have wanted since 1982
reviews.review_description:
My main memories of 1982 are playing 3D Monster Maze on the ZX81 and the UK network premiere of Alien on ITV shown immediately after the 1982 World Cup Final. How I longed for a game like 3D Monster Maze featuring a Xenomorph rather than a T-Rex…

I bought Alien: Rogue Incursion day one but waited for patches before diving into the game properly.

After the latest patch this is an excellent VR game with an outstanding Aliens atmosphere - literally the best Aliens game I have played on any format for capturing the nail biting tension of being chased by a Xenomorph.

The plot is just ‘good enough’  and lifted by some quality voice acting (not quite Half-Life Alyx quality, but similar format).

I have also been pleasantly surprised by the level of VR immersion. I was expecting just gunplay and ladder climbing, but there are many more mechanics implemented throughout.

As for the graphics, the patches have improved things over the initial release and I think they are on a par with other AAA titles on the Quest 3.

All in all, this is a great release and a longtime VR dream come true - cannot wait for Part 2!

After sleeping on this due to negative reviews I can honestly say this game is a great vr experience, if you like the aliens franchise and fps with a sense of dread this is for you. I love the way the gun locks in to you without this I would have died more often. Just using the scanner gives vibes of alien isolation but with much more immersion. Second best alien game ever after the original arcade version

Game play is great fun, always felt a level of anxiety throughout. I want to give it 5 stars but the number of times I died because my hands were not controlling the weaponry correctly got irritating. Meta just released a controller update which made it even worse, not just this game either. Not sure why people are complaining about save points, there are loads throughout the levels, you just need to use the map and learn your locations. Some parts are supposed to be difficult, otherwise wheres the fun in it?

I cant for the life of me figure out the intel achievement. I completed all 4 and it seems to generate two extra labelled as "???". I cant find any info, anywhere, about them 🤷‍♂️
I've played it through like three times on the hardest difficult now. Had a lot fun playing, felt very immersed and I still manage to get scared when one of the aliens gets me by surprise

Holy crap, this game kept my vellus hairs on end with a well crafted mix of creepy environments, sound, and spot on recreations of the aliens. 

This was a great experience, and I can't wait for the second half!

This game has the perfect alien atmosphere, makes you feel the tensed constantly. When the radar randomly beeps up and you know an alien is nearby but you don't know exactly where is a superb feeling!
What could be improved is to maybe make the guns aim a bit better, because it can be sometimes pretty hard when you have to fight multiple aliens (I played the game on higher difficulty). But that would probably also decrease the tension during those fights as well, so I don't know. Leave it as it is 😊
Looking forward to the second episode.

Could they still release a patch, what happened, the PC and PS versions are still leading, interestingly, there is no difference between the versions in the Metro games, I don't understand. What happened here, why is there such a big difference in the meta version? Without a patch, the game is unplayable. I hope they are working on it so that the game is the same as on the PS or PC version.

Immersive and nice story although the graphics and the physics could have been better. The firing looks bad but the weapons are nice when you learn to load properly. I would like to see some melee options in order to react and gain time if being cornered. It can get quite challenging as you have three life bars but a single hit takes two and you have to use the med every time. Also, if you miss the offsprings, you just die. I tried playing on the hardest level and it is impossible as you need to master dodging. I do recommend it but not for the full price.
It’s so immersive!!! You will be terrified!!! Ofcourse it would be nice if the graphics were sharper but the game is still superb.

Just completed.The game itself and the gameplay was amazing.Its the perfect difficulty,I died a good few times and needed a few attempts to get past certain bits.The game keeps you wanting to play it every chance you get,and it's not a short game.Ive gave it 5/5,which is saying something about how good the game is as the graphics aren't spectacular but that could improve with future updates.This games definitely up there with the best of them on the quest

Alien: Rogue Incursion brings the iconic franchise into VR with a promising premise, but the Quest 3 version stumbles hard. The visuals are disappointingly low-res, with murky lighting and blurry textures that sap the atmosphere of any real tension. Interactions feel undercooked—weapon handling is serviceable, but environmental engagement is minimal and lacks polish. While the story has potential, the immersion is constantly broken by performance hiccups and graphical compromises. A missed opportunity that might fare better on more powerful platforms.
Love what I see but unfortunately I get nauseous playing games without teleport. I know it’s lame but is there anyway you can add this option?

From start to finish.....Great Adventure. I am an Alien's "groupie" from the original in the 70's. The detail and game play in this are superb. Based on some early reviews I know there had been some glitches but they are more than worked out now and I highly recommend this game to everyone.

Meta and game devs, what's going on? Did nobody play test this before allowing on Meta store??!!

I just bought this game on Quest 3 and the micro stutters are constantly every 3-5 seconds, like seriously???

I even used my 30% discount code too for this month so I guess that's wasted now! 😞

I'm going to refund this game after playing only 10 minutes, it really hurt my eyes with the extremely poor performance.

I own 100's of games including all latest titles like Batman, Metro and Arizona Sunshine 2 and have zero issues with performance in any other game on Quest 3 but this game is completely unplayable in its current state.

I'll update my review in future when these performance issues have been fixed. It does look like a really great game and I'd love to play it but the performance is shocking.

The trick is learning how to manage your moves. In other words, the logic of this game is being strategic. It's knowing how to navigate to safe save spots. But for crystal generation, you need an autosave.
